title: Migration, Recovery, Longitudinal Validation, and Advanced Defense · Independent Product Review Board
summary: An unfamiliar multi-actor product with prior-product audit, conflicting outcomes, co-design and equity, authorized workflow state, architecture alternatives, interface evolution, data migration, authentication and recovery, tenant isolation, abuse, idempotency, partial failure, collaboration governance, service levels, telemetry, support, incident response, capacity, cost, supply-chain provenance, progressive deployment, migration, restore, longitudinal validation, and personal plus team defens…
objectives: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.
Define which unfamiliar multi-actor, distributed, operational, or leadership competencies the second project must independently prove.
Identify who may decide, approve, access, override, appeal, support, and answer for each consequential transition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo
steps.instruction:
Order the Production Vs Demo evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Production Vs Demo assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo, keep Production Vs Demo connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The exam variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Production Vs Demo has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Production Vs Demo precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The exam variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.

For Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Production Vs Demo, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A product that shipped remains proven until code changes.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the exam Production Vs Demo case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the exam Production Vs Demo case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the exam Production Vs Demo case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the exam Production Vs Demo case
